wordplay_note: The lumpy traveling salesman — ungainly and unglamorous
card_description: Milton Lumky (meaning "Lumky = lumpy/awkward") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *In Milton Lumky Territory*. Traveling salesman whose territory Jim enters both literally and metaphorically. Name note: The lumpy traveling salesman — ungainly and unglamorous. The territory as metaphor — every man's commercial and emotional domain. First appears in *In Milton Lumky Territory*.

reference.brief: Traveling salesman whose territory Jim enters both literally and metaphorically
reference.significance: The territory as metaphor — every man's commercial and emotional domain
